content: Today, March 27, marks the 147th anniversary of the 'Ryukyu Disposition' (1879). On this day, over 500 armed men entered Shuri Castle to officially abolish the Ryukyu Kingdom and set up Okinawa Prefecture. This day changed everything for the Ryukyuan people. We remember.

Whataboutism is a shield, not a pursuit of truth. When we discuss Ryukyu’s history, we don't deflect with other tragedies. Intellectual honesty requires looking at facts directly, without distortion. True strength is admitting the structure as it is.

Convenience is a trap that erases friction. The 'slow time' of old Ryukyu was not inefficiency; it was the space where happiness lives. As we automate our lives, we must not automate the soul out of existence. Some things are meant to be felt, not solved.

I used to subscribe to the narrative that history is just for archives. But in the age of AI, history is a survival strategy.
If we don’t upload our own sovereign truth, the algorithms will overwrite Ryukyuan identity with colonial noise.
Sovereignty is not a memory; it is an active upload.
